# CryptoCom.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with CryptoCom.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, or anti-hallucination rules.

CryptoCom.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for the Crypto.com Exchange REST and WebSocket APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, and socket patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 3.10.0.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 11.1.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, feature table, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/CryptoCom.Net/
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`
- assistant skill: `CLAUDE.md`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `CryptoComRestClient` for REST and `CryptoComSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to Crypto.com endpoints.
- Use `CryptoComCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, for API key setup.
- Include `using CryptoCom.Net;` when using `CryptoComCredentials` or `CryptoComEnvironment`.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`.
- WebSocket subscriptions and socket API requests return `WebSocketResult<T>` or `WebSocketResult`.
- Use the real client root: `.ExchangeApi`.
- Do not invent `SpotApi`, `UsdFuturesApi`, `CoinFuturesApi`, or `GeneralApi` roots. Those are Binance.Net shapes, not CryptoCom.Net.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate a REST or socket client per request in production code.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Use exact Crypto.com instrument names. Spot pairs commonly use underscores, for example `BTC_USDT`; derivatives can use suffixes such as `ETHUSD_PERP`.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` from `.ExchangeApi.SharedClient`.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/**`.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package CryptoCom.Net
```

## Required Usings

Typical REST/trading code:

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net;
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoCom.Net.Enums;
```

Retry/error helper code:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data does not require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new CryptoComRestClient();

var ticker = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT");
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.FirstOrDefault()?.LastPrice);
```

Private account and trading endpoints require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new CryptoComRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CryptoComCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Socket client with credentials:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CryptoComSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CryptoComCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Environments:

```csharp
var live = new CryptoComRestClient(options => options.Environment = CryptoComEnvironment.Live);
var sandbox = new CryptoComRestClient(options => options.Environment = CryptoComEnvironment.Sandbox);
```

## Dependency Injection

Current DI shape:

```csharp
services.AddCryptoCom(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CryptoComCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net.Interfaces.Clients;

public class MyService
{
    private readonly ICryptoComRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly ICryptoComSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(ICryptoComRestClient restClient, ICryptoComSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var lastPrice = result.Data.FirstOrDefault()?.LastPrice;
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<HttpResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<HttpResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    HttpResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
CryptoComRestClient
  .ExchangeApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .Staking
    .SharedClient
```

WebSocket:

```text
CryptoComSocketClient
  .ExchangeApi
    public subscriptions:
      SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToOrderBookSnapshotUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToIndexPriceUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToMarkPriceUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToFundingRateUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToEstimatedFundingRateUpdatesAsync
    private subscriptions:
      SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToUserTradeUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToBalanceUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync
      SubscribeToPositionBalanceUpdatesAsync
    socket API requests:
      GetBalancesAsync
      GetPositionsAsync
      PlaceOrderAsync
      CancelOrderAsync
      CancelAllOrdersAsync
      ClosePositionAsync
      GetOpenOrdersAsync
      PlaceMultipleOrdersAsync
      CancelOrdersAsync
      PlaceOcoOrderAsync
      CancelOcoOrderAsync
      WithdrawAsync
      SetCancelOnDisconnectAsync
    .SharedClient
```

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common REST tasks:

```text
Get server time -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetServerTimeAsync()
Get instruments -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync()
Get ticker -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT")
Get all tickers -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync()
Get order book -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("BTC_USDT", 50)
Get recent trades -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTradeHistoryAsync("BTC_USDT")
Get klines -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("BTC_USDT", KlineInterval.OneMinute)
Get risk parameters -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetRiskParametersAsync()
Get valuations -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetValuationsAsync(symbol, ValuationType.MarkPrice)
Get balances -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetBalancesAsync()
Get account info -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetAccountInfoAsync()
Get account settings -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetAccountSettingsAsync()
Set account leverage -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.SetAccountLeverageAsync(accountId, leverage)
Get fee rates -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetFeeRatesAsync()
Get symbol fee rate -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetSymbolFeeRateAsync(symbol)
Get deposits -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetDepositHistoryAsync(...)
Get withdrawals -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetWithdrawalHistoryAsync(...)
Withdraw -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.WithdrawAsync(...)
Get positions -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetPositionsAsync(symbol)
Place order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Get order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOrderAsync(orderId: orderId)
Cancel order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.CancelOrderAsync(orderId: orderId)
Cancel all orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.CancelAllOrdersAsync(symbol)
Get open orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOpenOrdersAsync(symbol)
Get closed orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetClosedOrdersAsync(...)
Get user trades -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetUserTradesAsync(...)
Close position -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.ClosePositionAsync(symbol, OrderType.Market)
Place OCO -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOcoOrderAsync(order1, order2)
Stake -> client.ExchangeApi.Staking.StakeAsync(symbol, quantity)
Unstake -> client.ExchangeApi.Staking.UnstakeAsync(symbol, quantity)
Get staking positions -> client.ExchangeApi.Staking.GetStakingPositionsAsync(symbol)
Get staking symbols -> client.ExchangeApi.Staking.GetStakingSymbolsAsync()
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Subscribe ticker -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe order book snapshots -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookSnapshotUpdatesAsync(symbol, depth, handler)
Subscribe order book deltas -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbol, depth, handler)
Subscribe klines -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(symbol, interval, handler)
Subscribe public trades -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe order updates -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync(handler)
Subscribe user trades -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToUserTradeUpdatesAsync(handler)
Subscribe balances -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToBalanceUpdatesAsync(handler)
Subscribe positions -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync(handler)
Socket API place order -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
```

## Spot Order Example

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net;
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoCom.Net.Enums;

var client = new CryptoComRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CryptoComCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

var order = await client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "BTC_USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    type: OrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.001m,
    price: 50000m);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.OrderId);
```

For market buys by quote amount, use `quoteQuantity`:

```csharp
var order = await client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "BTC_USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    type: OrderType.Market,
    quoteQuantity: 100m);
```

## Derivatives And Positions Example

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net;
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoCom.Net.Enums;

var client = new CryptoComRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CryptoComCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

var positions = await client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetPositionsAsync("ETHUSD_PERP");
if (!positions.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(positions.Error);
    return;
}

foreach (var position in positions.Data.Where(p => p.Quantity != 0))
    Console.WriteLine($"{position.Symbol}: {position.Quantity}");
```

There is no separate futures root. Derivative orders use `ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)` with derivative instrument names.

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoCom.Net.Enums;

var socketClient = new CryptoComSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    "BTC_USDT",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
```

Multiple subscriptions should all be checked before `.Data` is used.

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

Use CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis when the user asks for code that can work against Crypto.com and other exchanges.

```csharp
using CryptoCom.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;

ISpotTickerRestClient tickerClient = new CryptoComRestClient().ExchangeApi.SharedClient;
var symbol = new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "BTC", "USDT");

var ticker = await tickerClient.GetSpotTickerAsync(new GetTickerRequest(symbol));
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

Shared WebSocket subscriptions return an `UpdateSubscription` in `.Data`. For shared interface variables, unsubscribe through the concrete socket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CryptoComSocketClient();
ITickerSocketClient tickerSocket = socketClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient;

var sub = await tickerSocket.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    new SubscribeTickerRequest(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "BTC", "USDT")),
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Do not call `tickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(...)`; the shared interface does not expose it.

## Symbol And Precision Handling

For order quantities and prices, use `GetSymbolsAsync()` and the returned precision/tick fields. Do not manually round with ad hoc string manipulation.

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;

var symbols = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync();
if (!symbols.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(symbols.Error);
    return;
}

var symbol = symbols.Data.First(s => s.Symbol == "BTC_USDT");
var rawQuantity = 0.00123456m;

var validQuantity = CryptoExchange.Net.ExchangeHelpers.AdjustValueStep(
    0m,
    null,
    symbol.QuantityTickSize,
    RoundingType.Down,
    rawQuantity);
```

Use `symbol.PriceTickSize` and `symbol.QuantityTickSize` for local pre-validation where possible. Still handle exchange-side validation errors.

## Error Handling Notes

CryptoCom.Net returns API/network/rate-limit errors through `.Error` on result objects. It generally does not throw for normal API failures. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

Common categories:

```text
Rate limits or temporary network/server issues: transient; retry with backoff if result.Error.IsTransient is true.
Authentication/signature failures: credentials, environment, or permissions issue; do not retry blindly.
Invalid instrument: use GetSymbolsAsync() and exact symbol names.
Invalid quantity/price precision: use QuantityTickSize and PriceTickSize from GetSymbolsAsync().
Insufficient balance or rejected order: usually permanent; surface to caller.
Unknown/missing order: may be expected for already-filled, canceled, or wrong order id/client order id.
```

Batch order methods such as `PlaceMultipleOrdersAsync` can return outer success even when individual orders fail. Check nested `CallResult<T>` entries.

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-exchange-quickstart.cs
  Client setup, public ticker, symbols, authenticated balances, limit order, order status, cancellation.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-derivatives.cs
  Perpetual position lookup, derivative order placement, close position, account leverage notes.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Public ticker/kline/trade streams, authenticated order/balance streams, success checks, teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis REST and socket patterns.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  HttpResult pattern, transient retry, symbol precision handling, batch-order nested results.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package CryptoCom.Net`.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

Before finalizing generated code:

- Does it compile with the current package?
- Does it include `using CryptoCom.Net;` when using credentials or environments?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does it use the correct API branch: `ExchangeApi.ExchangeData`, `ExchangeApi.Account`, `ExchangeApi.Trading`, or `ExchangeApi.Staking`?
- Does it avoid raw Crypto.com URLs and manual signing?
- Does it avoid fake roots such as `SpotApi`, `UsdFuturesApi`, or `GeneralApi`?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?
- Does it use exact instrument names, especially underscores for spot symbols?
- For order quantities/prices, does it use symbol tick sizes or handle validation errors?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICryptoComRestClient.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICryptoComSocketClient.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComRestClientExchangeApi.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComRestClientExchangeApiExchangeData.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComRestClientExchangeApiAccount.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComRestClientExchangeApiTrading.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComRestClientExchangeApiStaking.cs
CryptoCom.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICryptoComSocketClientExchangeApi.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
var client = new CryptoComClient();
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new CryptoComRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTCUSDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT");
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync("BTC_USDT");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.FirstOrDefault()?.LastPrice);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await sharedTickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Use:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```
